item.value: How to increase SEO for your clients
Created by Wimba Create, licenced to South Central College

newpage.para: One of the ways a company can differentiate from the competition in today's world is to be first on the Google search page for their product/service category.
This tutorial will list several important tips that and your client can do to increase the SEO of their web pages.

newpage.heading: Spiders, Crawlers, and Robots
newpage.para: The most popular search engines such as Google and Yahoo use a program called a spider, web crawler, or robot. These names all mean the same thing. This type of program reads a web page and then follows all the links included on that page.

The spider extracts information from the site, using the HTML tags in the page to determine what is important. This information is stored in an index. When a person does a search the results that are displayed are pulled from this index based on mathematical formulas that determine what information is pertinent to the search string that was typed in.
newpage.heading: Ranking Search Results
newpage.para: How the results of a search are ranked are the key to SEO. Google changed the search engine rankings by using a "voting" formula. A web page receives a higher Google rank based on how many other web pages linked to that page and how important those web pages were. Having a link to your page on ibm.com gives you a higher Google rank than one from computers4Sale.com.

Since Google's inception they have been continuously improving their search formulas (algorithms). Many of the concepts used by Google are now being adapted by other search engines as well. This tutorial highlights some of the things that you can do to get your clients' sites the highest rank possible for specific searches based on ethical and acceptable practices.

para.b: Here's some of the things your client can do to increase the SEO of their site:

list.item: Think long term when registering domains

newpage.heading: Titles are everything.
newpage.para: Write short snappy, attention getting titles that include keywords that relate to the content of the page. Write your titles first and then the copy. A good title makes a promise a promise and then delivers. The title makes the promise. The content that follows delivers.
Stay focused

Know your target audience and write to meet their needs. Keep in mind that the only reason people will keep reading your page is if they are getting something of value. Use bulleted lists to help people scan and still catch the highlights of the page.

para.b: Keep it short and sweet. Less is more.
Include links that relate to what your customer will want.
More information? More details? Other alternatives? Think about the links that add value to the pages you look at out on the Web.

newpage.para: Don't use the non-descript "click here". Instead write copy that describes the link and make that copy the link. Which link would you use?

Click Herehttp://performancing.com/promotion/3-ways-to-immediately-increase-search-engine-traffic vs. How to write effective linkshttp://performancing.com/promotion/3-ways-to-immediately-increase-search-engine-traffic

para.b: Link to the other pages in your site

newpage.para: Include lots of links to other pages on your site, not just in the menus. This will give you a better link ratio. For example, if your home page talks about the history of your Gizmo(r) product, link to the product page showing the current model. This will make your link profile more trustworthy and ensure that the spider indexes all of the pages in your site.

Links to your privacy policy and shipping rates on every page will lower your link value. Each time a spider comes to a repeating link it thinks that link is more important. You can exclude these pages from redundant searching by using a robot.txt file. (See below.)

Keep those pages up-to-date. This is one of the criteria that search spiders use to determine ranking: when was the page last updated. In this fast changing world a page that was last updated six months ago is as valuable as a six month old newspaper.

Blogging is a great way to keep the site dynamic. A blog is an ongoing journal which encourages new content to be put out on the site. Many sites have a blogging space in the middle with sidebars with the regular, more stable information.

newpage.pagetitle: Think long term when registering domains

para.b: Register your site for 5-10 years or longer

newpage.para: Although not mentioned often this could become more important in the future. In 2005 Google applied for a patent that uses the expiration date of the domain name as one of their ranking criteria. The next time you purchase or renew your domain name, extend the date for 5-10 years. This gives your site much more legitimacy than if the domain is only around for another year or two.
As domain names become more and more scarce (and more and more valuable) this makes even more sense.

para.b: What happens if your domain registration doesn't get renewed?

newpage.para: The industry is filled with stories of companies not getting their domain re-registered for some reason or other. People around the world watch the expiration lists hoping to pick up a valuable domain name that can later be resold, sometimes for tens of thousands of dollars.
When your business loses its domain name you can no longer get email and your customers will see someone else's web page (or no web page) until you get it back.

I know, you've seen these really nice web sites that use Flash introductions. But, there's a price to pay here. If you are really serious about SEO, don't make Flash the focus of your site. Search engines will not index the content inside a Flash object. Sure, the designer can put in a few search strings, but you can't be certain that is being done and the search engines are not written to analyze Flash objects, they are written to analyze pure text.
Flash is best used for "movie" demonstrations. All the information that is given by the Flash object should also be represented in text form somewhere else on the page.
You may have the best looking pages in the world, but if you aren't showing up on the search engines then you won't have a lot of people coming to your site.

newpage.pagetitle: What you can do as a web programmer
newpage.para: Here's what you can do as a web programmer to increase the SEO of your clients' pages:

list.item: Increase the content ratio of the page
Use a unique title and description

newpage.heading: Keyword Ratio On a Page
newpage.para: Before Google turned the Web world upside down everyone mixed the styling of the page in with the content. If a designer wanted a dark blue headline using Arial font these were embedded as attributes inside the <h1> element.

Then web developers discovered that Google, and other search engines, calculated page importance by calculating how many keywords were in title, headings, and body of the page compared to the total character count.
All the sudden all those font= " " and color= " " attributes were watering down the importance of the page.
newpage.heading: Use CSS to Separate Content from Style
newpage.para: CSS - Cascading Style Sheets came to the rescue by allowing web designers to put the style information for a page in the <head> element of the page. This kept it separate from the content of the page which is located inside the <body> element.
By using CSS you are able to tell the browser specifically how things should be displayed. At the same time you will not water down the content of the page.

CSS also allows you to store your CSS code in another document completely. This gives an additional value by allowing you to share the same CSS code with all the pages in the site. Need to change a style, just edit one file and every page in the site updates in appearance automatically.
newpage.heading: Going Naked

para.b: Displaying a page without CSS code is called "going naked" and a good developer will create web pages this way.

newpage.para: First a designer starts with the content. The important point of the page is marked using the <h1> element. Headings that are secondary in importance are marked using the <h2> element. Each block of text is marked using either a <div> element or a <span> element. The <div> is used to mark blocks of code and the <span> element is used to mark a section of code inside another line or block of code, like special line in a paragraph.

This creates a plain-text document that uses elements to simply designate an outline showing what text is very important (the headers) and what is supportive text (the paragraphs and the alt=" " attribute for each image.).
This makes the page very accessible to non-visual browsers such as search spiders and people with visual disabilities.
Next, the CSS code is added in the <head> section of the page or in an external file.
This does not affect the search spiders but does make the page more interesting and easier to read (we hope) for the user.

newpage.pagetitle: Use a unique title and description
newpage.heading: Use a pertinent title and a succinct description.

para.b: Probably one of the most important parts of every page is the <title> element and the description meta tag.

newpage.para: The search spiders use these two page elements to determine the keywords for the page. The words used in the title should also be in the description as well as throughout the content of the page.

Here's an example: Imagine creating a page that talks about the 60th anniversary of a company. The page has historical photos and shows the user how much the company has changed, especially highlighting its newest product, the Gizmo.

But, if the title is "A Big Day" and the description is "A great time was had by all at our celebration" the search engines won't rank the site near as high as if you would have used "60th Anniversary Celebration Highlights Growth of the Gizmo" and the description read "The 60th Anniversary Celebration highlights the historical growth of our new product line from simple wire based communications to the newest addition, The Gizmo"

para.b: I've highlighted the keywords so you can imagine what is in the contents of this page.

newpage.heading: Valid web pages rank higher
newpage.para: By writing to XHTML standards you are making the search engine's job much easier. Using the rules of XML documents it knows that every start tag has a stop tag and every attribute is surrounded by quotes. It only has to look for lower case tag names.
By making certain every page passes TidyUI or the W3C Validation you are increasing the SEO for that page. That means zero errors and warnings.
newpage.heading: Limit the Use of Tables
newpage.para: Tables are very tag heavy. Keep tables to a minimum by using CSS to position your blocks of code on the page. For the best SEO you want the fewest number of tags and the greatest amount of content.
You can further remove code bloat by moving all JavaScript to an external file. This is very similar to putting CSS in external files.
newpage.heading: Use Text Links
newpage.para: Text links are always easier for a search engine to index. Graphic links, JavaScript links, and Flash all inhibit search engine spider programs. Instead, use CSS to make your text links "pretty" and interesting to the user.
newpage.heading: Always provide a site map
newpage.para: A site map describes how the site is laid out. You can submit your sitemap to Google which will give the search engine more information about your site. In essence you will be assisting Google's spider programs by helping update the Google index.
There are many sitemap generators including sitemapgenhttp://sourceforge.net/project/showfiles.php?group_id=137793&package_id=153422 out on SourceForge.

newpage.heading: Keep the URL of a page simple.
newpage.para: Don't use "&id=" as a parameter in your URLs. Google will ignore these address links and not include them in their index.

para.b: For example: This page won't be included in the Google index

newpage.heading: Tell the Search Spiders or Robots Where to go
newpage.para: This file tells crawlers which directories can or cannot be crawled. Make sure it's current for your site so that you don't accidentally block the search engine programs.

One use of the Robot.txt file is to keep certain files from being indexed. This is a great trick while you are developing a site and have the pages on a temporary server. Don't forget to change the Robot.txt file when the site or page goes live!

para.b: Here's an example of this file:

htmlinclude.pre: # /robots.txt file for http://webcrawler.com/ # mail webmaster@webcrawler.com for constructive criticism User-agent: webcrawler Disallow: User-agent: lycra Disallow: / User-agent: * Disallow: /tmp Disallow: /logs

newpage.para: The lines beginning with # are comments.
The * in the third User-agent is a wild-card which means every robot visiting.
newpage.heading: You can also include information in the meta tag for individual pages:
newpage.para: This meta tag in the <head> element will keep the page from being indexed. The nofollow keyword also tells the search spider not to follow any links on the page:

para.b: Make certain this is removed when the page goes live or the SEO for the page will forever remain ZERO!

newpage.para: Google has a free tool that will analyze your Robot.txt filehttp://www.google.com/support/webmasters/bin/answer.py?answer=35237.

para.b: Google has put together an extensive set of free tools to help you determine:

list.item: The information Google has about the site
Who is linking to your pages
The current cache of your site
Other pages that are similar to yours in the Google index

image.alt: Screen shot showing some of Google's sitemap tools

newpage.para: Here is the link to Google WebMaster Toolshttps://www.google.com/accounts/ServiceLogin?service=sitemaps&nui=1&continue=https://www.google.com/webmasters/sitemaps/siteoverview%3Fhl%3Den&hl=en. You have to register with Google, but the tools are free.
This is a great way to get a before and after picture of a site to show your client how the changes you've made to the site have increased their ranking.
(These may become out of date, this is the nature of the Web. If so, do a search for keywords like: SEO techniques)
CopyBloggerhttp://www.copyblogger.com/why-you-should-always-write-your-headline-first/ - Why you want to write your headline first.
The SEO Bookhttp://www.seobook.com/ - A new entry every day. Many, many great ideas for improving SEO
Google's WebMaster Guidelineshttp://www.google.com/support/webmasters/bin/answer.py?answer=35769 - An excellent checklist to increase the SEO for a site.
Web Robots FAQhttp://www.robotstxt.org/faq.html - Using Robot.txt and other useful ideas

para.b: This completes the tutorial: Web Development - SEO

newpage.para: May 2009
Written by Peter K. Johnson, Instructor
South Central College
North Mankato, MN 56003
Peter.Johnson@southcentral.edu
